title: When the Lighthouse Stood Still
synopsis: A young unicorn discovers that the lighthouse at the edge of the world has stopped turning, and the wind has mysteriously disappeared. In a breathless countdown from five to zero, Stardust must find a way to restore the natural order before everything stands still forever.

pages.text: The lighthouse beam stopped moving. Just stopped. Right in the middle of its sweep across the sea. The young unicorn with a pearly-white coat galloped closer. Her rainbow-pastel mane whipped behind her. But wait. No wind. No wind at all. Her mane hung perfectly still. The lighthouse keeper's dinner sat cooling on the windowsill. Steam should be rising from the soup bowl. But the steam froze in place like tiny white fingers. Something was terribly wrong at the edge of the world.

illustration.description: A young unicorn with a pearly-white coat, a rainbow-pastel mane and tail, a glowing spiral horn, and shimmering silver hooves approaches a tall lighthouse. The lighthouse beam is frozen mid-sweep. Steam from a soup bowl on the windowsill hangs motionless in the air. Cozy kitchen home art style with warm yellows and oranges, family gathering atmosphere, homemade food visible in the window.
illustration.alt: Stardust approaching the lighthouse with frozen steam and still lighthouse beam

pages.text: Stardust's silver hooves clicked against the lighthouse door. Click. Click. Click. The sound echoed strangely. Too loud. Too sharp. Even the ocean had stopped making noise. The waves hung like blue-green glass. The lighthouse keeper opened the door. His beard was stuck mid-flutter. His eyes were wide with worry. 'The wind died all at once,' he whispered. 'Everything just... froze.' Inside, his family sat around the dinner table. Forks halfway to mouths. Milk pouring from a pitcher but hanging in the air like a white ribbon.

illustration.description: A young unicorn with a pearly-white coat, a rainbow-pastel mane and tail, a glowing spiral horn, and shimmering silver hooves stands at the lighthouse door with the lighthouse keeper. Inside, a family sits frozen at a dinner table with suspended food and milk pouring in mid-air. Cozy kitchen home art style with warm yellows and oranges, family cooking together atmosphere, dining table scene, homemade food.
illustration.alt: The lighthouse keeper's family frozen at their dinner table with Stardust at the door

pages.text: The lighthouse stairs wound up and up and up. Stardust's horn began to glow brighter. Maybe magic could fix this. Maybe gentle unicorn magic could wake the wind. At the top, the great light sat dead still. No turning. No sweeping. No guiding ships home. The lighthouse keeper climbed behind her. His footsteps echoed like drums. 'It happened when my grandson put down his tablet,' he said. 'He was always swiping and tapping. Then he set it aside to eat dinner with us. And everything stopped.' The tablet lay dark on a small table. Screen black as night.

illustration.description: A young unicorn with a pearly-white coat, a rainbow-pastel mane and tail, a glowing spiral horn, and shimmering silver hooves climbs the lighthouse stairs with the keeper. At the top, a large motionless lighthouse beacon and a dark tablet on a small table. Cozy kitchen home art style with warm yellows and oranges, family gathering atmosphere, homemade food visible.
illustration.alt: Stardust and the lighthouse keeper at the top of the lighthouse with the still beacon and dark tablet

pages.text: Stardust touched her horn to the great light. Sparks flew everywhere. Gold and silver and rainbow colors. But nothing moved. The light stayed frozen. The wind stayed gone. 'Try the tablet!' called the keeper. 'Maybe that's the key!' The little unicorn's horn touched the dark screen. It flickered once. Twice. A game appeared. Moving colorful shapes. Spinning windmills. Swirling clouds. The tablet hummed with electric life. And suddenly. A tiny breeze stirred. Just a whisper. But it was something. The tablet's wind moved while the real wind slept.

illustration.description: A young unicorn with a pearly-white coat, a rainbow-pastel mane and tail, a glowing spiral horn, and shimmering silver hooves touches her horn to a tablet screen showing animated windmills and clouds. Sparks of gold and silver surround them. The lighthouse keeper watches hopefully. Cozy kitchen home art style with warm yellows and oranges, family cooking together atmosphere, homemade food nearby.
illustration.alt: Stardust touching her horn to the tablet screen with magical sparks flying

pages.text: The tablet wind grew stronger. Digital breezes swirled around the screen. But they couldn't reach the real world. Couldn't wake the frozen air. Stardust understood now. The magic balance was broken. Too much screen time. Then too little. The world didn't know how to breathe without the gentle rhythm. She pressed her horn against both. The tablet and the lighthouse beam. Bridging the gap between digital wind and real wind. Her magic flowed like golden honey. Connecting the two worlds. Teaching them to dance together again. Screen time and real time. Both needed. Both precious. Both part of the same beautiful dance.

illustration.description: A young unicorn with a pearly-white coat, a rainbow-pastel mane and tail, a glowing spiral horn, and shimmering silver hooves stands between a glowing tablet and lighthouse beacon, her horn touching both. Golden magic flows between them like honey. Digital and real wind swirl together harmoniously. Cozy kitchen home art style with warm yellows and oranges, family gathering atmosphere, homemade food elements.
illustration.alt: Stardust bridging tablet and lighthouse with golden magic flowing between them

pages.text: WHOOSH! The real wind woke up. The lighthouse beam began its steady sweep. The ocean waves crashed and rolled. Steam rose from the soup bowl again. The family downstairs laughed and clinked their forks. Dinner continued. But this time, the tablet glowed softly beside them. Not ignored. Not obsessed over. Just there. Part of the evening. Part of the family rhythm. The lighthouse keeper hugged Stardust's neck. 'You taught us the secret,' he whispered. 'Balance. Not too much. Not too little. Just right.' The wind sang through her rainbow mane. The lighthouse guided ships safely home. And everything moved in perfect, gentle harmony once more.

illustration.description: A young unicorn with a pearly-white coat, a rainbow-pastel mane and tail, a glowing spiral horn, and shimmering silver hooves stands with the lighthouse keeper as wind blows through her mane. The lighthouse beam sweeps normally, waves crash below, and through the window the family enjoys dinner with the tablet glowing softly nearby. Cozy kitchen home art style with warm yellows and oranges, family cooking together, steam and flour, aprons, everyday love moments, dining table, homemade food.
illustration.alt: Stardust with the lighthouse keeper as normal life resumes with balanced screen time

coverPrompt.description: A young unicorn with a pearly-white coat, a rainbow-pastel mane and tail, a glowing spiral horn, and shimmering silver hooves stands before a tall lighthouse. The lighthouse is eerily still against a calm sky. Cozy kitchen home art style with warm yellows and oranges, family gathering atmosphere, homemade food visible in lighthouse windows.
coverPrompt.alt: Stardust the unicorn looking up at a motionless lighthouse

questions.question: What happened to the lighthouse beam at the beginning?
questions.explanation: The lighthouse beam stopped moving right in the middle of its sweep across the sea.

questions.question: What was wrong with the wind?
questions.explanation: The wind died all at once and everything froze in place, including steam and waves.

questions.question: How did Stardust try to fix the problem?
questions.explanation: Stardust used her glowing horn to touch both the lighthouse light and the tablet, bridging them with magic.

questions.question: What did the lighthouse keeper's grandson do before everything stopped?
choices: He put down his tablet to eat dinner
questions.explanation: The keeper said everything stopped when his grandson put down his tablet to eat dinner with the family.

questions.question: What was the secret Stardust discovered?
choices: Balance between screen time and real time
questions.explanation: Stardust learned that balance is key - not too much screen time, not too little, but just the right amount.
